lesson: A beater that hums, stalls and trips with healthy windings but a dead run capacitor is the textbook test-before-you-swap fault. The motor is fine; condemning it is the expensive miss.
symptoms: Trips out after a few seconds.

tests.error-log: BEATER OVERCURRENT trips on every start attempt. Nothing else unusual in the log.
tests.inspect-beater: Beater turns freely by hand — no ice, no seizure, nothing jammed.
tests.continuity-test: Motor windings read in spec. The run capacitor measures a fraction of its rated microfarads.

symptoms: Loud hum then a click from behind the panel; the barrel never turns.
Worked fine at close last night.
Beater only starts if someone spins the drive by hand first.
Dies again on the next cycle.

flavour: Saved a nine-hundred-dollar motor with a twenty-dollar can of electrons.
authenticityNote: A dead run capacitor makes a perfectly healthy single-phase motor hum, stall and trip — the textbook test-before-you-swap fault (GDD §2.2). Condemning the motor is the classic expensive wrong answer.
